```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  n <- sample(5:12,1); p <- sample(seq(0.20,0.70,0.05),1); x <- sample(1:(n-1),1)
  ans <- dbinom(x,n,p)
  vals <- round(c(ans,p^x*(1-p)^(n-x),choose(n,x)*p^x,
                  dbinom(x,n,1-p),1-ans),5)
  if (length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.5f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Em `r n` ensaios de Bernoulli independentes, com probabilidade de sucesso `r p`, qual é a probabilidade de exatamente `r x` sucessos?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
Para $X\sim\operatorname{Binomial}(`r n`,`r p`)$,
$$P(X=`r x`)=\binom{`r n`}{`r x`}p^{`r x`}(1-p)^{`r n-x`}
=`r sprintf("%.5f",ans)`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S6Q2_pmf_binomial
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
